examples.body: > compensate for overhiring during the peak demand of the pandemic My dudes it is Q4 2025. How much longer is this excuse going to hold water?
No Hiring Boom during COVID. Hired 10K and 20K people in back-to-back years during COVID. I'm...not sure what to say here.

In WWII, they tried to figure out where to put armor on planes based on where they saw bullet damage. Problem is, they could only see planes that weren't shot down and successfully made it back, so the bullet holes actually represented places where a plane could survive a shot. O

You have to understand that a lot of decisions aren’t based on how good AI is. It just has to be good enough to convince the non-technical person making decisions at an organization. As you can probably guess, the bar there is pretty low.

Yep. A candidate’s incentive is to get the job. They’ll adapt to whatever paradigm to get the interview. My favorite is when Amazon or Microsoft asks if you have X experience in a drop down. When a negative is an auto reject what the hell do you think people will answer.

Harsh truth but it's the truth. It's even sadder to see people downvote you if you dunk on OP in their own thread. People actually gaslight others into abandoning their entire original career track from age 23-30 just to restart at the bottom of the ladder working 80hrs minimum.

A lot of people treat quant like a regular career pivot,but you’re often competing with people who’ve been obsessed with math and probability since high school.It’s not impossible to break in from another background....just way harder than most expect.
